title: Transitioning children to animal based eating and an active lifestyle

chunks.title: Transitioning to an Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The discussion begins with a personal experience about transitioning children to an animal-based diet, highlighting the challenges faced during this period. The parents removed junk food from the house and enforced a diet rich in healthy fats, meats, and organs despite initial resistance from their kids.
chunks.transcript: i remember you telling me the story of this time in your life when we first met and uh you just you described both stryker and rad just laid out on your graph they're just like completely they were so tired because i think this is the way to do it i don't have kids but um and i think that this you know pushes a lot of people's buttons they would say oh you can't you're starving your kids it's like no there's plenty of good food for them to eat they're just not choosing to eat it because they're so addicted to the junk food and it's your kids basically revolted right they didn't they didn't eat for a week because they chose not to eat the good food that's what happened right and they were just like so tired and eventually their brains were like we have to eat it was like you guys gave him an ultimatum and you said no more junk food this is what you're eating and it was all the good food with the fats and the meats and the organs nose to tail and they said heck no and you said well we'll see who breaks first right is that kind of how it went too much but um we got rid of all the food right all the process crap right like that's gone there's no option right so we knew it was a matter of time and i can't tell you how many times we would put some bone broth with some liver inside of it maybe maybe uh um there's a few other things in the bone broth and they wouldn't touch it and it's like okay guess what you're seeing that for the next meal and the next meal and so sure over a couple of days i mean they were they were starving and then they would have a bite and they loved it you know and now they think it's the best it's the best thing ever you know and now i think if you put liver in front of them they they want to say they love it they don't dislike it it's just that they know that liver and bone marrow comes first they know that nose-to-tail stuff comes first and then they get to eat some more stuff that they really enjoy but back then there was just no option like it wasn't in the house we weren't going anywhere and and so and i knew we weren't giving it i remember one time barbara said to me she's like do you think we're doing the right thing you know it's been this many days they're not eating they're like zombies they're acting like they have the flu do you think we're do and the truth is i wasn't real sure but i knew i had to be ultra sure for her and i said we're absolutely doing the right thing look at how many times they stop breathing look at how many times we've had to use the epipen look at how many ambulances have come to take our kids from us because we could lose them and now we have an opportunity to reset that we get a redo and thank god we did what we did you know because the way that they are now man i mean you already knows it know this they're kicking ass in life and so this is one of the most important things we we could have done and i just i want to share that message with the world

chunks.title: Children's Adaptation to New Foods
chunks.summary: The speaker recounts how the children eventually embraced and even enjoyed foods like liver and bone marrow after initially refusing to eat them. This segment emphasizes patience and persistence in helping kids adapt to a more nutritious diet.
chunks.transcript: because there's so much opportunity you know for kids to have a decent chance at life and to really kick ass at life i think it's so impressive when i'm at your house and rad your youngest son just does a crossfit workout on his own like your wife nobody says like hey it's time for you he just goes in there and starts racking up plates and he's doing like you know he's doing like cleans and jerks and he's like doing walks and he's swinging kettlebells it's like he's just doing this on his own man he's just like he's got so disciplined now he's doing it and then he's like pulling a sled you know you got like you're you're you know your youngest kid just like pulling a sled on his own and at dinner you know i've hung out with you a lot of your house and i've seen your boys and they eat with us man they eat the organs with us they eat the meat with us and i mean stryker loves his sweet potatoes he loves his carbs but uh they they eat that stuff they eat the meat first they know they have to eat that stuff first like they're they're into it and uh they're clearly benefiting it's so cool to see them you know really coming into their own and thriving and it's cool to hear rad like the blood or because you said rad asked for seconds on the blood times many times he's asked for seconds he's asked for thirds and you know what we do is far more important than what we say right what we model is exponentially

chunks.title: The Importance of Modeling Healthy Habits
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to the significance of modeling healthy behaviors, as the speaker reflects on how children feel encouraged to eat nutritious foods when they witness their parents doing the same. The discussion emphasizes the role of parenting in instilling discipline and healthy habits.
chunks.transcript: important than what we say and what we teach and so you know sometimes the kids will do a workout and they'll say oh this was tough you know do i really got to work out again or eating this you know didn't really taste really good do i need to have this again and these are early conversations that we have our kids understand the value of earning stuff now and you don't have to enjoy everything you do in life if you enjoy everything you do in life everything becomes flat right you need to pay a price and you need to earn it and when you understand the benefits on the other side of that man everything is better everything tastes better everything smells better everything is better because you just got better because you paid that price and and being able to talk to them about that being able to shape that and then being able to see them take their own initiative and go do these things go pay that price on their own nobody has to tell my guys to work out you know every once in a while it might be christmas and my guys say it's christmas do i have to work out today and i say you don't have to you don't have to and then normally the next question is do we still get paid can we still get paid and of course the answer is of course you don't get paid but i bribe them too right and they get a dollar per workout and and if they complete all the workouts for the month and they've done their chores i doubled

chunks.title: Earning Healthy Choices
chunks.summary: The discussion covers the principles of earning rewards through hard work, such as exercising and making healthy food choices. The speaker shares a system of incentivizing workouts for kids, fostering a mindset that appreciates the value of effort and the benefits that come from it.
chunks.transcript: that money and so they get paid a decent amount of money and and so um if they miss one workout let's say it's the 20th day of the month then it starts over at zero and so they so they might not be feeling it that day but they know the principle behind it this is not supposed to be fun and we always talk about this and i love when when i say brad why do you work out and he'll tell me why and and he'll say and i said do you enjoy it and goes it's not called funning out it's called working out right it's not supposed and i love that they can say this stuff and so i'm glad that you you can testify to this you see you see them do it they take the initiative they go out and they do it and and and they live a better life because of it imagine how much greater it feels when you've worked out that hard and now you're going to go relax you know what it feels like to relax what about everyone else who's been relaxing they never worked for it and they're just relaxing right there's no juxtaposition right they've just been relaxing and lazy their whole life and i talk about this on the stories usually on thursday you're going to suffer one way or another right you can actively suffer and say you're going to pursue growth you can work out you can work hard you can earn it or you're going to passively suffer because your life sucks because you never knew the the peaks and the troughs so one way or another you're going to suffer why not suffer in a way

chunks.title: Perspective on Suffering and Growth
chunks.summary: The final segment delves into broader themes of suffering and personal growth. The speaker reflects on how individuals can choose to actively pursue growth through challenges instead of passively suffering due to inaction, framing it as a vital lesson for both the children and the audience.
chunks.transcript: that's going to create a permanently better life go earn that and so to see them do that is a valuable thing i want to see more people do that and this is why this is why really i'm on social media now is because the world needs to
